subject: [For Review] Jul CB Quarterly Management Meeting

content.answer:
Hi Ian/JH,

Please find attached deck for the upcoming Quarterly CB Management Meeting
on 15th Jul, Tuesday, for your review.
FP&A, CNLS and SIP sections are WIP (with yellow stickers) and will be
ready by 13th Jul, Sunday, for 2nd iteration.

As aligned, most content is similar to what was presented to Terence in the
June monthly meeting but with numbers refreshed, and with a few updates.
Key differences for review in this iteration are as follows:

*[CB LFF] Slide 72* - 1) Shortened CB FBS projection to Dec 26, instead of
3 years. 2) Inserted comparison with TTS LFF penetration
*[Counter TB] Slide 85* - Highlighted revision to TB program target vs
previous presentation to CF in June, due to 1) lower seller willingness to
onboard, 2) lower than expected progress from ERP initiative
*[Counter TB] Slide 91* - Illustration of structural price difference of
one similar TB vs Shopee SKU

Let me know if you have any questions.

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

For LFF section, we should have a total PC2 view of CB (SLS + FBS + 3PF)
now vs end of Dec? This is to clear the doubt whether higher LFF % will
curb CB business profibility.

Hi Ian/JH,

Please find v2 of the deck attached with updated content from CNLS and SIP.
FP&A on Slide 3 will be updated by tomorrow, 14th Jul, morning after data
is ready.

Key new content to review in this iteration is as below:
*[CNLS] Slide 67-72*: SOB Update, CIR Benchmark, SOB Defense Levers
*[SIP] Slide 111:* CNSIP Growth Plan
*[SIP] Slide 121*: Ads/Pricing tradeoff study

To Jianghong's question: Noted, will work with CB FP&A for PC2 projection
til Dec.

On Sat, Jul 12, 2025 at 5:50 PM Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>
wrote:


-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - We over-achieve on topline but miss on EBITDA?

Slide 35

   - On JIT, we need to explain how we will protect the buyer experience
   - And probably need to compare how our APT looks like vs UST sellers



Slide 45- 49

   - For the IP collaboration, I thought we narrowed it down to the 1 PH to
   quickly start with?

Do we have details on how the collaboration would look
   like yet?

- Right now, it feels very high level still and not much movement since
   we spoke with him



Slide 50 -64

   - What do we intend to show with this content?

Slide 71

   - Is FBS something that we can really convince CNLS sellers to join?

How
   does their APT compare against us?

- In general, I think we need to properly align on the few key levers
   that really push CNLS growth and double down on it across all markets?

What
   is it exactly?

- SSP participation
      - Paid Ads/AMS participation
      - LS/Video participation
      - Campaign participation
      - Etcetc
   - Otherwise, it feels like all markets are doing their own thing and it
   will be generally hard to track
   - How are we helping CNLS sellers migrate into other markets right now?
Is that something that we should do and what is the best way to get started?

Slide 86

   - I remember for VN, we were supposed to push harder for 3PF because TT
   was doing so?
Seems like we did not manage to do much here?

Slide 88

   - For TTS CB, are the % here current or?
- In general, we have 0 advantages vs TTS?
That is not good at all?

In
   fact we are quite behind them?

Slide 95

   - We adjusted targets quite drastically here?

Slide 102

   - On incentives, I thought we were supposed to have similar for all
   markets?
We are back at the stage where each market does its own thing?

- Can we get all markets to participate and really just focus on
   Fashion/Beauty/F&B?
- Why do we only get 720 leads/month on acquisition front?

That does not
   really get us anywhere from an impact point of view?

Slide 105

   - Lever 1 is just too slow right now?

Slide 106

   - This view is hard to understand we have a fairly standardize way of
   measuring price competitiveness right?

I cannot quite tell where the issue
   lies right now
   - I am not sure Taobao platform vouchers are very aggressive right now
   either?

Slide 107

   - Do not quite understand this view. Why is the pricing so much higher?
- So Taobao loses money on this or?
- We need to be comparing what a buyer pays right?

Then it is an apple
   to apple comparison?

- We should be comparing what a buyer pays on TB vs Shopee and then try
   to understand why our prices are so much worser?

Slide 108

   - Right now, for price match, do we match TB store to TB store?

Or TB
   store to CB store?

We should be negotiating for all CB stores if we want to
   be truly price competitive?

If we are just matching to their TB store, it
   is quite pointless from impact point of view



Slide 123

   - What is the rationale behind this?

*From:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Sunday, 13 July 2025 10:58 pm
*To:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>
*Cc:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Larry Lin
<larry.lin@shopee.com>; Cheng Derek <derek.cheng@shopee.com>; Elaine Wang <
elaine.wang@shopee.com>; Ruofei Wang <phyllis.wang@shopee.com>; Ning Li <
ning.li@shopee.com>; Chengchen Huo <chengchen.huo@shopee.com>; Jinyu Du <
jinyu.du@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Yuna Kwon <
yuna.kwon@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>; Jiahao Chen <
jiahao.chen@shopee.com>; Yiru Zhao <yiru.zhao@shopee.com>; Ray Wang <
ray.wang01@shopee.com>; Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Max Goh Shi
Long <max.gohsl@shopee.com>; Angela Hsu Yi-Han <angela.hsu@shopee.com>;
James Li [SZ] <james.li@shopee.com>; May Cai <may.cai@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [For Review] Jul CB Quarterly Management Meeting



Hi Ian/JH,



Please find v2 of the deck attached with updated content from CNLS and SIP.

FP&A on Slide 3 will be updated by tomorrow, 14th Jul, morning after data
is ready.



Key new content to review in this iteration is as below:

*[CNLS] Slide 67-72*: SOB Update, CIR Benchmark, SOB Defense Levers

*[SIP] Slide 111:* CNSIP Growth Plan

*[SIP] Slide 121*: Ads/Pricing tradeoff study



To Jianghong's question: Noted, will work with CB FP&A for PC2 projection
til Dec.



On Sat, Jul 12, 2025 at 5:50 PM Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>
wrote:

For LFF section, we should have a total PC2 view of CB (SLS + FBS + 3PF)
now vs end of Dec?

content.answer: SCS - Added in Slide 36 to highlight key levers to control JIT lead time
CNLS - Added Slide 71 onwards to share details on CNLS joining FBS

LFF - Added details on Slide 92 on TTS projected LFF% vs our target by
Dec'26

Counter TB - Removed price comp, and to deep dive on price comparison
between TBG and SHP in next iteration.

Please also see responses to Ian's question below:

Slide 4

   -

   We over-achieve on topline but miss on EBITDA?


   -

MP+SLS P&L: Although CB exceeded our GMV targets, it's primarily due
   to high GMV runrate from TH(108% RR) but with low margins of just 1% of
   GMV. This is offsetting the lower volume from MY(95% RR) but has higher
   margins of 5% of GMV
   -

Warehouse Ops P&L: missed target due to unexpected costs from PHD
   warehouse pre-termination and inventory migration


Slide 35

   -

   On JIT, we need to explain how we will protect the buyer experience
   -

   And probably need to compare how our APT looks like vs UST sellers

Added in P36 to highlight our key levers to control JIT lead time, and to
safeguard our buyer experience, meanwhile, also added a table to show our
order split by fulfillment type (LFF, VMI, JIT) to show that our overall
BWT will still be 1-1.2 days faster than MP sellers despite of launching
JIT, which will contribute only <2% of orders;



Slide 45- 49

   -

   For the IP collaboration, I thought we narrowed it down to the 1 PH to
   quickly start with? Do we have details on how the collaboration would look
   like yet?
   -

   Right now, it feels very high level still and not much movement since we
   spoke with him

Yes, we have narrowed down to PH and secured cooperation with the KOL
Jenela, who has 5 million followers. The estimated warm-up date is in
August, with the campaign launching in September and lasting until
November.

We reorganized the collaboration portion (P43 - P53), and added a summary
page P43 to highlight our plan and key progress



Slide 50 -64 (now Slide 54-68)

   -

   What do we intend to show with this content?

This is pretty much a fact based update per Chris’ request in last meeting,
we are showing him what is AE strategy for this and next year, and the
rationale behind;

Think the implication to us is that though SCS isn’t as important as AE
consignment to AE, but SCS is still serving with our strategic values by
building e2e supply chain capabilities; We are prepared and on standby to
scale whenever Shopee needs us, such as entering new markets, especially
those with strong AE/Temu presence. This would allow us to compete
effectively with more controlled products and pricing, and potentially
seller profile and 2. ops blockers:

CN CNLS sellers are highly concentrated on a few top sellers (top 10
   sellers take ~50-70% of CN CNLS portfolio ADO), which are reluctant to
   convert as they use WH joint ventures to connect with local
   resources/relationships.

Key blockers are: a) duplicate listing need for 铺货; b) service level; c)
   bulky items.

We will also work with local FBS teams to solve inbound issues and VAS
selections by EoY and target to convert multiplatform fulfilling and seller
own wh sellers as much as possible. The framework will be aligned with CB
FBS and each local market.

Cost advantage:

Local FBS is providing handling fee discounts and other fee rate discounts
to convert more items with cost efficiency. We are still assessing whether
the cost efficiency is sustainable in the long run by excluding free
trial/commission discount incentives.

Table 1: Cost efficiency items ADO%

   -

   Scenario 1: Consider free trial and fee discount


MY*

TH

VN*

PH*

CN CNLS

100%

48%

100%

100%

Local CNLS

100%

46%

100%

100%

*Free trial: MY/PH provide 6-month free trial for new FBS joiner, VN
provide 3-month free trial.


   -

   Scenario 2: Consider fee discount only

No free trial

MY

TH

VN

PH

CN CNLS

52%

48%

36%

18%*

Local CNLS

39%

46%

26%

2.8%*

*PH site filters out quite a lot of sellers if not considering free trial
due to ~97% seller own WH.

Service advantage:

For user experience, Local FBS is providing a much better APT comparing
with CNLS.

Table 2: APT comparison

MY

TH

VN

PH

Local FBS 1

0.60

0.50

0.60

0.54

CNLS 2

0.87

0.83

0.88

0.90

1 Local FBS APT provided by local FBS

2 CNLS APT as of June 2025




   -

   In general, I think we need to properly align on the few key levers that
   really push CNLS growth and double down on it across all markets? What is
   it exactly?
   -

      SSP participation
      -

      Paid Ads/AMS participation
      -

      LS/Video participation
      -

      Campaign participation
      -

      Etcetc
      -

   Otherwise, it feels like all markets are doing their own thing
New seller acquisition package to acquire SLS sellers
New VIP incentive package to grow existing top 3PF sellers

Push for loosening of internal Shopee 3PF compliance requirement, to
   allow more new sellers to onboard to 3PF (TTS compliance does not do
   similar gatekeeping to sellers)




Slide 88 (now slide 92)

   -

   For TTS CB, are the % here current or?

TTS% here is projected for Q4’25 TTS LFF% from CNCI team study



   -

   In general, we have 0 advantages vs TTS? That is not good at all? In
   fact we are quite behind them?


We aim to be higher than TTS in LFF% by Dec’26 across all markets.

   -

   TTS LFF growth was higher than us because TTS SKU structure is more
   short tail driven and TTS taking on more compliance risk
   -

   To achieve our target, we shall focus on resolve all short term
   addressable blockers listed and double down on seller acquisition




Slide 95 (now slide 99)

   -

   We adjusted targets quite drastically here?


Yes, the detailed breakdown is shown in Slide 101. Key drop comes mainly
from Lever 1 (New Seller Acquisition 28.4K to 5.4)  and Lever 3 (Sourcing
by ERP 137.2K to 40.1K).

   -

   Lever 1, driven by previous overestimation of seller willingness to join
   and acquisition team capacity.
   -

   Lever 3, driven by previous overestimation of mainly due to lowered SKU
   efficiency assumption compared to earlier estimation. (CNSIP SKU Efficiency
   reference was used instead of general CB SKU)



Slide 102 (now slide 106)

   -

   On incentives, I thought we were supposed to have similar for all
   markets? We are back at the stage where each market does its own thing?

On Incentives, the framework to adopt is the same across markets as listed
below.



Incentive Type

New Seller

Existing Seller

SKU Sourcing

Seller Commit

New SKU Upload

30-50# New SKU Upload per month

Incentive disbursed per SKU upload

Paid Ads Investment

50 - 200 USD per month

NA

Price

5-12 % off CFS/DD price according to platform policy

Shop

TH: changed their Counter TB incentive structure just last week in order
   to streamline all seller programs and as such remove the PRM and Exposure
   element, we will work with them to resume the incentive element to make
   sure TH remains an attractive site for TB Seller.

TW: program focuses on SKU% migration and requires sellers to commit
   paid-ads investment before co-funding the same amount in order to ensure
   the platform is not favouring CB sellers. We will assess the feasibility of
   streamlining to the framework without compromising platform considerations.



   -

   Can we get all markets to participate and really just focus on
   Fashion/Beauty/F&B?

On market participation, most markets in the program, except those with
licensing restrictions (refer to table below).

The key call out here is PH opted not to participate in
Furniture/F&B/Beauty due to high ADO/ADG multiples vs TB (~2.5X to 18X
across all categories) and therefore, this push is not prioritized.


Counter Taobao Program Roll-Out Status by Country

Category

SG

MY

TH

TW

VN

PH

Fashion

Launched in Dec’24

Launched in Dec’24

Launched in Dec’24

Launched in Dec’24

Launched in Dec’24

Launched in Dec’24

Furniture

Launched in Mar’25

Launched in Mar’25

Launched in Mar’25

Not participating due to low incremental potential as most assortments are
already covered by TW Local

Launched in Mar’25, with focus on CNLS due to VNCB customs restriction.

Not participating due to strong TB multiples

F&B

Launched in Jul’25

Launched in Jul’25

Not participating due to TISI license restriction

Not participating due to license restriction

Not participating due to license restriction

Beauty

Launched in Jul’25

Launched in Jul’25

Launched in Jul’25

Launched in Jul’25

Launched in Jul’25



   -

   Why do we only get 720 leads/month on acquisition front? That does not
   really get us anywhere from an impact point of view?


On the Acquisition, the number of leads per month is limited by SAI
capacity (6 HC, each reaching 120 leads per month). Will prioritize TB top
500 sellers in each category and then try to acquire non-top sellers as a
supplement.

   -

Low seller acceptance rate (10~16%) due to lack of success cases.
(Details can refer to 106).

categories to increase seller willingness to join SHP and source more SKUs




Slide 106 (now slide 110)

   -

   This view is hard to understand we have a fairly standardize way of
   measuring price competitiveness right? I cannot quite tell where the issue
   lies right now
   -

   I am not sure Taobao platform vouchers are very aggressive right now
   either?


We adopted the Reg BD’s view to look at price breakdown. Comparing at
listing price level (Price C), for all the matched SKU (33%) we are not
price competitive. With platform vouchers/BPSF considered, we are still not
price competitive. We are working with CNCI to deep dive if
Platform/Program Fees is competitive for SHP vs TBG
Logistic Fees is heavily subsidized by TB for TBG sellers

UE of TB Global program is currently negative


Slide 107 (Removed)

   -

   Do not quite understand this view. Why is the pricing so much higher?
   -

   So Taobao loses money on this or?
   -

   We need to be comparing what a buyer pays right? Then it is an apple to
   apple comparison?
   -

   We should be comparing what a buyer pays on TB vs Shopee and then try to
   understand why our prices are so much worser?


We will remove this slide and do a detailed deep dive from the Seller and
from the Buyer point of view.



Slide 108 (now slide 111)

   -

   Right now, for price match, do we match TB store to TB store? Or TB
   store to CB store? We should be negotiating for all CB stores if we want to
   be truly price competitive? If we are just matching to their TB store, it
   is quite pointless from impact point of view

Noted. We are currently matching both:
TB Store to TB Store

TB Store to CB Store (Those that carry similar SKU to top TB SKUs)

Our current priority is to focus on the Top 1k listing in TB and with
matching TB store, we will work to expand this to other CB sellers.


Slide 123 (now slide 126)

   -

   What is the rationale behind this?

The rationale for higher reliance on paid ads investment for SIP SKUs is
the lack of organic traffic exposure from platform

Low CSPU coverage with only 1.2% SKUs covered in MY market leading to
low traffic boosting from platform

Limited platform organic traffic as SIP SKUs are more LT in nature i)
lack of brand awareness ii) mass translation with less customised title
based on local demand
Will add above into the ppt slide

Hi Ian/JH,

Please find V3 of deck attached, with updated FP&A data. Key changes in the
content as below:
1) SCS - Added in Slide 36 to highlight key levers to control JIT lead time
2) CNLS - Added Slide 71 onwards to share details on CNLS joining FBS
3) LFF - Added details on Slide 92 on TTS projected LFF% vs our target by
Dec'26
4) Counter TB - Removed price comp, and to deep dive on price comparison
between TBG and SHP in next iteration.

Please also see responses to Ian's question
